content.problemStatement:
Let's denote the length of the Longest Increasing Subsequence of the array BBB by LIS(B)\mathbf{LIS}(B)LIS(B).

For a permutation AAA of integers from 111 to NNN we can define two arrays of integers PPP and SSS, both of length NNN: Pi=LIS(A[1:i])=LIS([A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai])P_i = \mathbf{LIS}(A[1:i]) = \mathbf{LIS}([A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i])Pi‚Äč=LIS(A[1:i])=LIS([A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äč]), Si=LIS(A[i:N])=LIS([Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,AN])S_i = \mathbf{LIS}(A[i:N]) = \mathbf{LIS}([A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_N])Si‚Äč=LIS(A[i:N])=LIS([Ai‚Äč,Ai+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč]).

Let's call a pair of arrays (P,S)(P, S)(P,S) viable\mathit{viable}viable, if there exists a permutation AAA with these arrays as PPP and SSS.

Given two arrays PPP and SSS of length NNN, check if the pair (P,S)(P, S)(P,S) is viable and if it is, find any permutation AAA with these arrays as PPP and SSS.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT ‚ąí-‚ąí the number of test cases. The description of test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚ąí-‚ąí the size of the arrays PPP and SSS.




The second line of each test case contains NNN integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč.




The third line of each test case contains NNN integers S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äč.





Output Format
For each test case print `Yes` if the pair (P,S)(P, S)(P,S) is viable and `No` otherwise. You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes` and `YES` will all be treated as identical).


In case of an affirmative answer, on the second line print any suitable permutation AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 100‚ÄČ000100\,000100000
